codeReview.summary: The fix correctly replaces strconv.Atoi with strconv.ParseInt(..., 10, 64) for all integer parsing in the captcha module. This prevents integer overflow on 32-bit systems when handling large user IDs. The implementation follows CLAUDE.md patterns and includes a comprehensive test case covering normal user IDs, large user IDs exceeding 32-bit range (2^31), MaxInt64 values, and negative channel IDs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill file already documents the integer handling pattern correctly: 'Use strconv.ParseInt(str, 10, 64) for user IDs, not Atoi' and 'Telegram user IDs can exceed 32-bit range'. The worker followed this pattern correctly.

Worker followed the bugfix-worker skill procedure correctly: read feature description, located the bug with Grep, implemented minimal changes, added test coverage, and verified with go test. All steps in the Work Procedure section were followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Grep for Atoi, Read exact lines, Edit to replace with ParseInt, add test case, run tests

sharedStateObservations.observation: The captcha module already had ParseInt for user ID parsing in two places (lines 1333 and 1571 for targetUserID). The fix completes this pattern by also using ParseInt for timeout and maxAttempts parsing, maintaining consistency across the module.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/modules/captcha.go lines 1333, 1571 already used ParseInt; lines 631, 749 were fixed to match

summary: Review completed successfully. All strconv.Atoi calls in captcha.go have been replaced with strconv.ParseInt(..., 10, 64). The test case covers edge cases including user IDs exceeding 32-bit max (2^31), MaxInt64, and negative channel IDs. All tests pass. The fix is minimal, follows existing patterns, and properly prevents integer overflow on 32-bit systems.
